circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: Yas Marina Circuit, a 5. 281 km track on Abu Dhabi's Yas Island designed by Hermann Tilke, has served as the venue for Formula One's season-closing Abu Dhabi Grand Prix since 2009. As the second Middle Eastern Formula One circuit after Bahrain, it preceded later additions in Qatar and Saudi Arabia.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: A street course encircling Albert Park Lake in Melbourne's Albert Park suburb, this FIA Grade 1 licensed venue serves as the permanent home of the Australian Grand Prix and hosts the Supercars Championship Melbourne SuperSprint alongside various supporting racing events annually.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located in Spielberg, Austria, this 4. 326 km circuit was originally established as Österreichring and served as the venue for the Austrian Grand Prix throughout the 1970s and 1980s. Following reconstruction and renaming, the track hosted the grand prix once more during the late 1990s and early 2000s.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: The Baku City Circuit is a street track in Azerbaijan's capital city, positioned along Baku Boulevard, with a 6. 003 km layout that ranks as the fourth-longest Formula One venue. The circuit hosted the 2016 European Grand Prix as its first F1 event, followed by the Azerbaijan Grand Prix beginning in 2017, both operated by the Baku City Circuit Operation Company.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Opened in 2004, the 5. 412 km Bahrain International Circuit hosts the annual Formula One Grand Prix, which was historically significant as the first F1 race held in the Middle East. The FIA Grade 1-licensed venue accommodates various racing series including GP2, drag racing, and endurance competitions, and features multiple circuit configurations for different events.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, this 4. 657 km Spanish motorsport facility features a balanced layout combining extended straights with diverse corner complexes that test multiple racing disciplines. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: The Belgian racing facility near Stavelot spans 7. 004 kilometers and has served as the home of Formula One's Belgian Grand Prix since 1925, including participation in the inaugural 1950 F1 season. With the exception of 2003 and 2006, the circuit has continuously hosted the Grand Prix annually from 1985 onward.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Notre Dame Island in Montreal, the Circuit Gilles Villeneuve is a 4. 361 kilometre track that serves as the home of Canada's Formula One Grand Prix. Beyond its Formula One role, the venue has accommodated various international racing series including sportscar championships and open-wheel competitions.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: The Shanghai International Circuit in China's Jiading District serves as the primary venue for the Chinese Grand Prix, which took place annually from 2004 to 2019 before resuming in 2024. Beyond hosting this marquee Formula One event, the facility has expanded its motorsport calendar to include electric racing competitions beginning in 2024.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Located in Northamptonshire, England, Silverstone Circuit has hosted the British Grand Prix since 1948 and famously staged the inaugural World Championship race in 1950. After decades of alternating with other UK venues, the Grand Prix became a permanent fixture at Silverstone from 1987 onwards, establishing it as the traditional home of Britain's Formula One event.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: Located near Budapest in Mogyoród, the 4. 381 km Hungaroring hosted the 1986 Hungarian Grand Prix, marking the first Formula One race held in Eastern Europe. Built in just eight months, faster than any other F1 circuit, the track was constructed outside the city near a highway after government authorities rejected plans for a street circuit in Budapest's central park.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: The Autodromo Nazionale Monza is a 5. 793 km Italian racing venue constructed in 1922 near Milan, ranking as mainland Europe's oldest purpose-built circuit and the world's third overall. Home to the Italian Grand Prix since 1949 (with a single interruption in 1980), the track earns its nickname "Temple of Speed" from its characteristic long straights and fast-sweeping bends.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit is a 5. 807 km Japanese motorsport venue operated by Honda Mobilityland in Mie Prefecture, with a capacity for 155,000 spectators. The track is renowned for hosting prestigious Formula One races alongside the Japanese Super Formula championship series.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street course that winds through Paradise, Nevada, following sections of Las Vegas Boulevard lined with major hotels and casinos. The layout incorporates several iconic Las Vegas landmarks, such as the MSG Sphere, Caesars Palace, Bellagio, and Paris Las Vegas, as it traverses the famous entertainment district.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: Located in Mexico City, the 4. 304 km Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez honors two legendary Mexican racing brothers who both died in racing accidents. The venue has hosted Formula One's Mexican Grand Prix during multiple eras, most recently hosting the event continuously from 2015 onwards under its current "Mexico City Grand Prix" designation, following a 23-year absence from the championship calendar.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: The Miami International Autodrome is a temporary circuit constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens that hosted the Miami Grand Prix beginning in the 2022 F1 season. Spanning 3. 363 miles with 19 turns, the track was engineered by specialist designers and produces qualifying speeds averaging 139 mph.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Monaco Grand Prix takes place on a 3. 337 km street circuit that winds through Monte Carlo and La Condamine districts, circling the harbor of the principality. Although officially named Circuit de Monaco, it is widely known as Monte Carlo because the majority of the track runs within the Monte Carlo neighborhood.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: Located in Dutch coastal dunes approximately 35 kilometers west of Amsterdam, Circuit Zandvoort is a 4. 259 km track that hosted the Dutch Grand Prix continuously before departing the Formula One schedule, then made a comeback in 2021. The venue will continue to feature the championship's Dutch round through 2026, after which its Formula One agreement concludes.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: The Lusail International Circuit sits north of Doha in Qatar and spans 5. 419 kilometers with a prominent main straight measuring 1. 068 kilometers.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Interlagos, a 4. 309 km circuit in São Paulo, Brazil, opened in 1940 and was renamed in 1985 to commemorate Formula One driver José Carlos Pace, who perished in a 1977 aviation accident. The counterclockwise track features an adjacent kart facility dedicated to another Brazilian motorsport legend.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: A street circuit in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, situated along the Red Sea waterfront, the Jeddah Corniche Circuit has been the venue for the Formula One Saudi Arabian Grand Prix since its debut in 2021. The track also began hosting Formula E racing in 2025.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: Marina Bay Street Circuit is a street-based racing venue located in Singapore, with its layout running around the Marina Bay waterfront and spanning the Downtown Core and Kallang districts.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: The Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya near Barcelona features a 4. 657-kilometer layout known for its balanced mix of high-speed straights and diverse corner configurations, making it suitable for testing multiple aspects of car performance. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 status and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: The Circuit of the Americas in Austin, Texas is a 3. 426-mile FIA Grade 1 facility that annually hosts the United States Formula One Grand Prix alongside major racing events including MotoGP's Americas round and the Lone Star Le Mans endurance race. The venue has also accommodated numerous other motorsport championships throughout its operation, ranging from NASCAR to various international sports car series.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Alexander Albon is a dual Thai-British driver who currently races for Williams in Formula One, competing under the Thai flag.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: The Spanish driver competes for Aston Martin in Formula One, having secured two world championship titles during his time with Renault in 2005 and 2006, along with 32 race victories over 23 seasons. Beyond single-seaters, Alonso has achieved remarkable success in endurance racing, including the 2018-19 World Endurance Championship and multiple victories at prestigious events like Le Mans and Daytona, making him the sole driver to win both Formula One and World Endurance championships.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Italian driver Andrea Kimi Antonelli joined Mercedes in 2025 and has secured five Formula One victories since entering the championship.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman is a British Formula One driver currently racing for the Haas team.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Brazilian driver Gabi Bortoleto currently races in Formula One representing Audi, competing at the highest level of motorsport after establishing himself through junior racing categories.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Finnish driver Valtteri Bottas currently races for Cadillac in Formula One, bringing extensive experience from his previous work with Mercedes where he secured two runner-up finishes in the drivers' championship. Across his 13-year career in the sport, Bottas has claimed 10 Grand Prix victories.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Argentine driver Franco Alejandro Colapinto currently races in Formula One as a member of the Alpine team.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Jack Doohan, an Australian driver, holds a reserve role with Haas in Formula One while competing in European Le Mans Series endurance racing. He has made limited Formula One appearances, taking part in seven Grand Prix events between 2024 and 2025.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: Pierre Gasly is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Alpine. He achieved his sole Grand Prix victory at the 2020 Italian Grand Prix while driving for AlphaTauri.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Alexandre Hadjar is a French-Algerian driver who races for Red Bull Racing in Formula One, competing under the French colours.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: British driver Lewis Hamilton currently competes for Ferrari in Formula One, where he shares the record for most championship titles with seven wins alongside Michael Schumacher. He also holds numerous performance records including the most race victories, pole positions, and podium finishes throughout his career.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: German driver Nicolas Hülkenberg currently races in Formula One for Audi. Beyond his single-seater commitments, he achieved success in endurance motorsport by winning the 24 Hours of Le Mans in 2015 driving for Porsche.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson is a New Zealand-born racing driver currently competing in Formula One for the Racing Bulls team.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: The Monégasque driver has secured nine victories across his Formula One career while competing for Ferrari, where he finished second in the 2022 world championship standings. Leclerc continues to race for the Scuderia as he pursues further success in the sport.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Danish driver Kevin Magnussen, known as K-Mag, competed in Formula One across two separate periods from 2014 to 2020 and again from 2022 to 2024. Currently, he races for BMW as a factory driver in the World Endurance Championship with WRT, while also competing part-time in NASCAR's Cup Series for Trackhouse Racing in the No. 91 Chevrolet.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris, a British driver competing for McLaren, clinched the 2025 Formula One World Drivers' Championship with the team. Across his eight seasons in the sport, he has accumulated 11 Grand Prix victories.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: French driver Esteban Ocon currently races for Haas in Formula One, bringing with him experience from multiple teams throughout his career. He achieved a notable victory at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix while competing for Alpine, marking a significant milestone in his racing record.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Checo Pérez is a Mexican driver competing for Cadillac in Formula One, bringing extensive experience from 15 seasons in the sport. He finished second in the 2023 drivers' championship while with Red Bull and has secured six Grand Prix victories throughout his career.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Australian driver Oscar Piastri currently races for McLaren in Formula One, having secured nine Grand Prix victories since joining the sport.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Daniel Ricciardo, an Australian driver known by the nickname "the Honey Badger," competed in Formula One over 14 seasons from 2011 until 2024, during which he secured eight Grand Prix victories before retiring from the sport.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: George Russell, a British driver currently racing for Mercedes, has secured seven Grand Prix victories during his eight seasons competing in Formula One.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Carlos Sainz is a Spanish driver currently racing for Williams in Formula One, where he has claimed four race victories throughout his 12-season career in the sport.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: Logan Sargeant is an American racing driver who previously competed in Formula One during the 2023 and 2024 seasons. He has since transitioned to sports car racing, currently competing in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 category for Proton Competition, following earlier stints in prototype and endurance racing.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian motorsport competitor currently racing in Formula One for the Aston Martin team.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Yuki Tsunoda is a Japanese driver who has been active in Formula One since 2021, currently functioning as a reserve driver for both Red Bull Racing and its sister team Racing Bulls.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: Dutch-Belgian driver Max Verstappen competes for Red Bull Racing and has secured four consecutive Formula One world titles from 2021 through 2024. Over his 12-season career, he has accumulated 71 Grand Prix victories.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu is a Chinese motorsport driver currently working as a reserve driver for Cadillac in Formula One. He competed in the championship from 2022 to 2024 and holds the distinction of being the sole Chinese driver to have participated in F1 competition.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: The Enstone-based Alpine F1 team, formerly operating as Renault's factory squad, competes under a French licence with Mercedes power units. Since rebranding in 2021 to spotlight Renault's Alpine sports car marque, the team secured a single victory through Esteban Ocon at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix before Renault's championship withdrawal at the end of 2025.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin has engaged in Formula One competition through multiple corporate entities over time, with its present incarnation operating as AMR GP Limited under the Aston Martin Aramco Formula One Team banner.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari represents the racing arm of the Italian luxury automaker and serves as Formula One's oldest continuous competitor, participating in every championship since the sport's inaugural 1950 season. The team holds the distinction of being the most accomplished outfit in the championship's history, earning its iconic "Prancing Horse" nickname from its distinctive emblem.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: An American Formula One outfit founded by NASCAR team owner Gene Haas, the squad entered the sport in 2016 and has been guided by team principal Ayao Komatsu since early 2024, following the departure of longstanding leader Guenther Steiner.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: Founded by Peter Sauber in 1970, this Swiss engineering firm became a Formula One constructor in 1993 and remained in the sport for over three decades, making it the fourth-longest-serving team in the championship's history before ceasing operations in 2025. The organization previously achieved success in endurance racing, including a Le Mans 24 Hours victory, before Audi acquired its assets in 2024 to establish its own F1 programme.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren is a British racing outfit headquartered in Woking that competes in Formula One under its Mastercard partnership. The team operates as part of the broader McLaren Group and is owned by Bahrain's Mumtalakat investment fund.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: Mercedes has maintained a prominent presence in Formula One since 1954 through team ownership and engine supply, with its current incarnation based in Brackley and operating under a German racing licence. The team, now known as Mercedes-AMG Petronas, became jointly owned by the Mercedes-Benz Group, Ineos, and Toto Wolff from January 2022. Mercedes-affiliated teams are traditionally nicknamed the "Silver Arrows.

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One constructor that began competing in 2024 as part of Red Bull's two-team operation. Based in Faenza with a secondary facility near Milton Keynes, the squad functions as a sister team to the flagship Red Bull Racing outfit within the Austrian conglomerate's motorsport structure.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian-based Formula One team and constructor that entered the championship in 2024 as part of Red Bull's multi-team structure. Operating from Faenza with a secondary facility in Milton Keynes, the squad operates as Red Bull's second constructor alongside the primary Red Bull Racing outfit.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing is an Austrian-licensed Formula One team headquartered in England and owned by the Red Bull conglomerate. Founded in 2005 and managed by Christian Horner until his 2025 departure, the team is now led by Laurent Mekies, while Red Bull GmbH also operates the Racing Bulls team in the championship.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering is a British Formula One constructor founded in 1977 by Frank Williams and Patrick Head, succeeding Williams's earlier failed racing venture. Based in Grove, Oxfordshire, the team competes in 2026 under the Atlassian Williams F1 Team name and has established itself as a significant force in the sport's history.
